Localizable.After inserting text, Dictate watches (v:
After inserting text, Dictate watches (via Accessibility) how you edit it.  If you keep correcting "cloud code" to "Claude Code", it learns that and  applies the correction itself next time.

Everything stays on this Mac — the corrections are never uploaded, and they  apply no matter which cleanup mode you're using. Reset them any time below.
Localizable.Anchored to a corner or edge, the pill g: Anchored to a corner or edge, the pill grows inward toward the centre of the screen.
Localizable.Apple Intelligence isn't available on th: Apple Intelligence isn't available on this Mac, so this step will always be skipped.
Localizable.Applied by whichever step ends up cleani: Applied by whichever step ends up cleaning your text — "use British spelling", "keep my bullet points", and so on.
Localizable.Audio is never stored, and speaker label: Audio is never stored, and speaker labels stay in the file — they never reach the text you paste.
Localizable.Change the hotkey, language, or micropho: Change the hotkey, language, or microphone anytime from the menu bar icon → Settings.
Localizable.Cleanup is off — only filler words are r: Cleanup is off — only filler words are removed.
Localizable.Cleanup is off. Filler words are still r: Cleanup is off. Filler words are still removed — that costs nothing and never needs a model.
Localizable.Cleanup punctuates, capitalizes, and res:
Cleanup punctuates, capitalizes, and resolves the false starts you make  when you change your mind mid-sentence. ("Um" and "uh" are stripped no  matter what — that costs nothing and never needs a model.)

Out of the box this is just Apple Intelligence: free, on-device, no key,  nothing leaves your Mac.

Each step is tried in order, and the first one that works wins. A step  with no API key isn't an error — it simply isn't the step that cleans  your text, and Dictate moves on to the next one.

Add a cloud provider and it goes *above* Apple Intelligence, because the  on-device pass almost always succeeds — anything underneath it would  never get a turn. So the ones you add are tried first, and on-device  catches whatever they missed.

You only ever see "cleanup failed" if *every* step failed — and even then  your transcript is inserted unchanged rather than lost.
Localizable.Click the icon for Settings, the Welcome: Click the icon for Settings, the Welcome Guide, and recent dictations.
Localizable.Dictate lives in your menu bar — look fo: Dictate lives in your menu bar — look for the microphone icon.
Localizable.Dictate needs these to hear you and type:
Dictate needs these to hear you and type for you.
Everything stays on this Mac.
Localizable.Dictate runs invisibly. Everything still: Dictate runs invisibly. Everything still works:
Localizable.Dictate starts hidden and waits for your: Dictate starts hidden and waits for your hotkey.
Localizable.Dictate uses **hold Fn** as its push-to-: Dictate uses **hold Fn** as its push-to-talk key. macOS also assigns the Fn key its own action — set it to **Do Nothing** so the two don't collide.
Localizable.Dictate watches how you fix its text and: Dictate watches how you fix its text and stops making the same mistake.
Localizable.Every step failed, so your transcript go: Every step failed, so your transcript goes in unchanged — and you get told.
Localizable.Filler words go; everything else lands e: Filler words go; everything else lands exactly as you said it.
Localizable.Fn currently changes your input source: Fn currently changes your input source
Localizable.Fn currently opens the emoji picker: Fn currently opens the emoji picker
Localizable.For the global hotkey, and to type into: For the global hotkey, and to type into other apps
Localizable.Hide it and Dictate runs invisibly — ⌃⌥⌘: Hide it and Dictate runs invisibly — ⌃⌥⌘, always brings Settings back.
Localizable.Hold a key. Speak. Your words appear — i: Hold a key. Speak. Your words appear — in any app.
Localizable.Hold the key and speak; let go and your:
Hold the key and speak; let go and your words are inserted wherever the  cursor is. If you'd rather not hold it down, tap it once to lock dictation  on and tap again to stop.

Fn sits under your left thumb and does nothing else in most apps, which is  why it's the default. Pick Right ⌘ if you use Fn for something else —  switching input sources, say, or the emoji picker.
Localizable.Hold to dictate, release to insert. A qu: Hold to dictate, release to insert. A quick tap locks dictation on.
Localizable.Hotkey, menu bar, launch at login: Hotkey, menu bar, launch at login
Localizable.How large the transcript is drawn inside:
How large the transcript is drawn inside the pill. The pill (and the  invisible window behind it) grow to fit, so nothing gets clipped at the  larger sizes.

The transcript is always trimmed to the last stretch of what you said, so  a long dictation scrolls rather than sprawling across your screen.
Localizable.How long new words take to land as you s: How long new words take to land as you speak.
Localizable.How long the pill takes to change state: How long the pill takes to change state — listening to cleaning up.
Localizable.How quickly recognized words settle into:
How quickly recognized words settle into the pill. This fires continuously  while you talk, so it defaults faster than the state transitions.

ASAP puts words up the instant they're recognized. Slow-mo takes its sweet  time about it. Neither affects how fast the text is actually inserted —  this is purely how the popup reads.
Localizable.I have read and agree to the Terms of Se: I have read and agree to the Terms of Service.
Localizable.In Keyboard settings, set “Press 🌐 key t:
In Keyboard settings, set “Press 🌐 key to” → “Do Nothing.”
Prefer a different key? Pick Right ⌘ later in Dictate’s Settings.
Localizable.Live on-device transcription with no net: Live on-device transcription with no network round-trip — it works on airplane mode.
Localizable.Mail, Slack, your editor — anywhere you: Mail, Slack, your editor — anywhere you can type, you can dictate.
Localizable.Model IDs change often — type whichever: Model IDs change often — type whichever one you want to use.
Nothing dictated in the last hour.
Localizable.On-device speech recognition (SpeechTran: On-device speech recognition (SpeechTranscriber) is only available on macOS 26 or later.
Localizable.Permission needed — enable Microphone an: Permission needed — enable Microphone and Speech Recognition in System Settings
Localizable.Permissions, your hotkey, and a box to t: Permissions, your hotkey, and a box to try it in
Please read and accept to continue.
Localizable.Polishes punctuation and removes false s: Polishes punctuation and removes false starts, entirely on this Mac. Free, and needs no key.
Localizable.Punctuation and false starts fixed, enti: Punctuation and false starts fixed, entirely on this Mac.
Localizable.Recognition runs on-device in the langua: Recognition runs on-device in the language you pick. System default follows your Mac's language, and whichever microphone macOS is currently using.
Localizable.Same, on a model you host yourself. Noth: Same, on a model you host yourself. Nothing leaves your machine.
Localizable.Same, plus any custom instructions you g: Same, plus any custom instructions you give it. The transcript goes to %@; a typical dictation costs a fraction of a cent.
Localizable.Saves a transcript to this Mac while you:
Saves a transcript to this Mac while you dictate, written to disk moments  after the words are spoken, with speakers told apart. It works best with  two or three voices.

Each file ends with a record of the session: which microphone was used,  when it started and stopped, and how long it ran. Times are your local  time, with the UTC offset attached, so the day of the week is the one you  actually lived and the exact instant is still unambiguous.

No audio is ever stored, nothing is uploaded, and the speaker labels  appear only in the file — they never show up in the text Dictate pastes  for you. On by default; turn it off and nothing is written at all.
Localizable.Selected language isn't supported for on: Selected language isn't supported for on-device recognition
Localizable.Speech is transcribed on your Mac's Neur: Speech is transcribed on your Mac's Neural Engine. Audio and text never leave this machine.
Localizable.Speech model download failed — check you: Speech model download failed — check your connection and retry
Localizable.Tap the hotkey to keep dictation running: Tap the hotkey to keep dictation running hands-free; tap again to stop and insert.
Localizable.The Fn key is free — you're good to go: The Fn key is free — you're good to go
Localizable.The menu bar icon is the visible way to:
The menu bar icon is the visible way to reach Settings, the Welcome Guide,  and your recent dictations.

Hiding it disables none of that, because the app's controls are keyboard  shortcuts rather than menu items:

• Your hotkey still starts and stops dictation.
• ⌃⌥⌘V still opens recent dictations.
• ⌃⌥⌘, still opens this window.

Those work system-wide whether the icon is showing or not, so you can't  lock yourself out by turning it off.
Localizable.The pill crossfades when it changes stat:
The pill crossfades when it changes state: from listening, to "Cleaning  up…", to an error. Normal is a little over a quarter of a second, which  reads as a transition rather than a flash.

Instant means genuinely no animation, not a very fast one — the pill simply  swaps.
Localizable.The pill grows to fit — bigger text mean: The pill grows to fit — bigger text means a taller pill.
Localizable.The pill that floats over your screen wh: The pill that floats over your screen while you dictate.
Localizable.The popup you see while dictating: The popup you see while dictating
Localizable.Tried top to bottom. The first step that: Tried top to bottom. The first step that works cleans your text.
Localizable.Turn on Apple Intelligence polish in Set: Turn on Apple Intelligence polish in Settings to remove false starts and fix punctuation — still fully on-device.
Localizable.Welcome Guide. Closes settings and opens: Welcome Guide. Closes settings and opens the guide.
Localizable.Where the pill sits on screen. Bottom ce:
Where the pill sits on screen. Bottom centre is the default, out of the way  of most windows.

When the pill is anchored to a side, it hugs that edge and extends toward  the middle of the screen as the transcript gets longer — rather than  growing out in both directions and running off the edge.
Localizable.While you're dictating, a small pill flo:
While you're dictating, a small pill floats over your screen showing what's  been recognized so far and how loud you are. It never takes focus, so it  can't steal your keystrokes.

The styles differ only in how they draw your voice: level bars are the  quietest, the voice orb and sonar ripple are the liveliest, and the  breathing halo drops the indicator entirely and lets the pill itself glow —  which leaves the most room for the transcript.
Localizable.Works with any OpenAI-compatible server: Works with any OpenAI-compatible server — Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, MLX, vLLM. Everything stays on your machine; if the request fails, your transcript is inserted unchanged.
Localizable.Write a speaker-labeled transcript to di: Write a speaker-labeled transcript to disk as you dictate.
Localizable.Your key is stored in the Keychain, neve: Your key is stored in the Keychain, never in preferences. Transcripts are sent to %@ to be cleaned; audio and transcription stay on this Mac. If the request fails, your transcript is inserted unchanged.
Localizable.Recent dictations — expand one to copy a: Recent dictations — expand one to copy any cleanup stage

InfoPlist.NSMicrophoneUsageDescription: Dictate listens to your voice to type what you say. Audio never leaves this Mac.
InfoPlist.NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription: Dictate transcribes your speech on-device. No audio or text is sent anywhere.
